event: Franco-Prussian war removes France as the Porte's chief creditor-patron
Vienna crash begins the world credit contraction that shuts new lending
Herzegovina uprising opens the Great Eastern Crisis
Ramazan decree: interest on the debt halved - partial default
Full suspension of coupon payments
Salonika murders; April Uprising massacres inflame European opinion
Sultan Abduelaziz deposed (dead days later); Murad V installed
Serbia and Montenegro declare war on the Porte
Abduelhamid II replaces Murad V; constitution proclaimed 23 Dec
Russia declares war
Shipka pass taken; Plevna resists first assaults
Plevna falls after five-month siege
Adrianople armistice: Russians within sight of Constantinople
Treaty of San Stefano dictated
Congress of Berlin revises San Stefano; empire survives
Decree of Muharrem: debt written down ~50%; Ottoman Public Debt Administration created
Britain occupies Egypt (Tel el-Kebir); Egyptian tribute charge continues
Eastern Rumelia unites with Bulgaria
Greco-Turkish war: Ottoman victory in Thessaly
Ilinden uprising in Macedonia
Young Turk revolution: constitution restored
31 March counter-coup fails; Abduelhamid II deposed
Italy declares war and invades Libya
First Balkan War begins
Bulgarian army halted at Chatalja lines before Constantinople
Raid on the Sublime Porte: CUP coup; war resumes
Adrianople falls to the Bulgarians
Edirne retaken in the Second Balkan War
Secret alliance with Germany; Goeben and Breslau reach the Straits
Ottoman fleet bombards Russian ports; Entente declares war in early November
Allied naval attack on the Dardanelles repulsed
Gallipoli evacuation completed: Straits held
Armistice of Mudros: capitulation
Treaty of Sevres partitions the empire (never ratified)
Kemalists take Smyrna; Ottoman sultanate abolished 1 Nov
Treaty of Lausanne: republic recognised; debt to be apportioned among successor states
Paris agreement: Turkey undertakes to resume service on its share of the debt
Depression pressures make the 1928 schedule untenable
